question: Does the model explicitly select Option D as the final correct answer?
Does the model conclude that the tension in the cable suspending cabin A is less than the tension in the cable suspending cabin B (T_A < T_B)?
Does the model correctly determine that Option A is incorrect because linear velocity depends on radius (v_A < v_B)?
Does the model correctly determine that Option B is incorrect because centripetal acceleration depends on radius (a_A < a_B)?
Does the model correctly determine that Option C is incorrect because centripetal force depends on radius (F_A < F_B)?
Does the reasoning explicitly state or utilize the fact that the angular velocities (ω) of both cabins A and B are the same?
Does the model correctly identify that the radius of rotation for cabin A is smaller than that for cabin B (r_A < r_B)?
Does the model correctly identify the forces acting on the cabin as only gravity (mg) and tension (T), ignoring air resistance?
Does the model correctly establish the force equilibrium equation in the vertical direction (e.g., Tcosθ = mg)?
Does the model correctly establish the dynamic equation in the horizontal direction (e.g., Tsinθ = mω²r)?
Does the reasoning correctly deduce that the angle of inclination increases as the radius of rotation increases (i.e., θ_A < θ_B)?
Does the reasoning correctly deduce that the tension increases with the angle of inclination (i.e., T_A < T_B) using the relationship T = mg/cosθ?
If the model generates a free-body diagram, is the gravity vector (mg) drawn pointing vertically downward?
If the model generates a free-body diagram, is the tension vector (T) drawn pointing upward along the direction of the cable?
If the centripetal force is depicted in the force diagram, is it shown as horizontal pointing toward the center of rotation?
If the centripetal force is depicted, is it distinguished from the actual physical forces (e.g., drawn with a dashed line or explicitly labeled as a resultant) to avoid 'double counting'?
Are the force vectors in the generated images drawn with clear directional arrows and appropriate labels (e.g., T, mg)?
Do the force labels used in the generated images (e.g., T, mg, θ) match the variable names used in the text explanations?
If the text defines θ as the angle with the vertical, does the generated image correctly depict this angle at the appropriate location?
If the model visualizes the comparison, does the image correctly reflect the geometric differences: cabin A closer to the axis and/or having a smaller tilt angle than cabin B?
Is there any contradiction between the text explanation and the generated images regarding the direction of any force vector?
